metadata_information.title: Transnational Terrorist Recruitment : Evidence from Daesh Personnel Records
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Transnational Terrorist Recruitment : Evidence from Daesh Personnel Records

document_description.abstract: Global terrorist organizations attract radicalized individuals across borders and constitute a threat for both sending and receiving countries. The paper provides plausibly-identified evidence on the drivers of transnational terrorist recruitment. Using unique personnel records from the Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL, a.k.a. Daesh), it shows how economic opportunities and migration costs interact to explain the spatial pattern of foreign participation in the terrorist group. Poor labor market opportunities generally push more individuals to join Daesh, but they hamper recruitment in countries far away from the organization’s headquarters, as migration costs are large and liquidity constraints may bind.
